mentions.note: Leading nuclear reactor supplier for military subs/carriers and commercial power; 170-year history, 70-year nuclear experience; tripled revenue/earnings as 10-year public company; positioned at intersection of national security, energy security, and AI data center power demand.
Real estate services company torn to pieces despite tremendous quarter; sold off on vague AI fears about office reduction lacking credibility.
Design software company able to rally after good quarter; shows floor exists for companies more insulated from AI disruption.
Down 33% YTD but trades below 15x earnings; multiple compression risk from AI efficiency gains impacting per-user SaaS pricing.
Hit 'hideous' declines after Anthropic's cybersecurity release; but Cramer defends at close—Claude can't replace threat detection/response mission; issue is PE multiple, not fundamentals.
Held since early 2000s Unocal deal, already up significantly; Cramer believes can go higher; strong 3.85% dividend; hold for continued energy demand upside.
Data monitoring/analytics platform rallied after good quarter; more insulated from AI disruption fears than broader enterprise software.
Pawn and credit company trading 15x earnings; no tariff or AI disruption risk; well-run operation printing money.
Financial data provider down 34% YTD; mission-critical tool (used on Mad Money) and not easily replaced by AI; multiple compression from unfounded fears.
Well-positioned for long-term energy demand growth; Cramer holds big position in charitable trust; best positioned for future nuclear and power plant deployment.
Leading military shipbuilder ($16B); best Navy play with 30-year shipbuilding plan visibility; 'been a buy...going to continue to be right'; not expensive.
Trading at 30x earnings with excessive crypto exposure (~50% of business); will remain volatile and trade like Bitcoin until balance shifts toward equities/bonds.
Fell 13% (worst since 2000) on Anthropic cybersecurity threat; not all AI announcements are credible; market may be overreacting.
Specifically targeted in Citrini Research AI apocalypse paper; facing multiple compression on fears of client self-sufficiency with AI.
Down 60% from all-time high; BDC redemption gating, suspensions, and controversial asset sales raise concerns about private credit health; Cramer is 'a seller, not a buyer of fraught situations.'
Trading at under 16x earnings (cheapest in years) due to AI fears; AI threat is borderline non-existent in online brokerage; current valuation is a steal.
Digital finance platform down 30% from $26; at current valuation with strong growth rate might be worth reconsidering but wait for more clarity.
Legal database down 39% YTD as Anthropic enters legal market; Cramer sees panic selling—real AI threat to legal research is overstated.
Data center cooling and power infrastructure; best play for AI-driven data center build-out; just had big jump, could pull back but 'Vertiv is the best, I love Vertiv.'
Enterprise software hit by broad AI-driven multiple compression; real earnings impact unclear but valuation pressure is real.
